pairs.question: When and where was Abū ‘Abdillāh ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Nāṣir al-Sa’dī born?
pairs.answer: Abū ‘Abdillāh ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Nāṣir al-Sa’dī was born on the 12th of Muḥarram in the year 1307 AH in the town of ʿUnaizah, located in the al-Qaṣīm region of Saudi Arabia.

pairs.question: What notable event occurred in Abū ‘Abdillāh ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Nāṣir al-Sa’dī's early life when he was seven years old?
pairs.answer: When he was seven years old, his father passed away, leaving him orphaned.

pairs.question: At what age did Abū ‘Abdillāh ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Nāṣir al-Sa’dī memorize the Qurʾān?
pairs.answer: He memorized the Qurʾān thoroughly by the age of eleven.

pairs.question: What were the primary academic fields in which Abū ‘Abdillāh ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Nāṣir al-Sa’dī authored books?
pairs.answer: He authored more than 40 books primarily in the fields of tafsir (Qurʾān exegesis), fiqh (Islamic jurisprudence), and 'aqidah (Islamic creed).

pairs.question: At what age did Abū ‘Abdillāh ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Nāṣir al-Sa’dī begin teaching?
pairs.answer: He began teaching at the age of twenty-three.

pairs.question: What significant educational role did Abū ‘Abdillāh ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Nāṣir al-Sa’dī assume in the year 1351H?
pairs.answer: In the year 1351H, he was placed in charge of education in his hometown, ʿUnaizah, serving as a mentor for all the students.

pairs.question: How did Abū ‘Abdillāh ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Nāṣir al-Sa’dī balance learning and teaching throughout his life?
pairs.answer: He dedicated his entire time to both learning from scholars and teaching others. This dual pursuit continued until he assumed a formal leadership role in education in ʿUnaizah.
